items.title: Lost but unharmed: Four women found safe after Bukit Tunggul hike
items.summary: SEPANG: Four women who became lost while hiking at Bukit Tunggul were found safe and brought out of the area by firefighters today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the hikers had lost their way during the climb. "All four women were successfully located by firefighters and escorted to a safe area.

items.title: Produce evidence of alleged Negri Sembilan plot - Asyraf
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has rejected allegations that the party was involved in efforts to destabilise or bring down the Negri Sembilan state government, said its secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. Asyraf said Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) had provided support to the state government until the expiry of its mandate, and had remained committed to ensuring administrative stability throughout that period. "Umno has never betrayed the commitments that were agreed upon," he said.

items.title: Khind expands rent-to-own push with first physical store launch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Khind Malaysia has launched its first physical rent-to-own (RTO) store in Semenyih, expanding its consumer financing model beyond digital platforms as it continues to evolve from its roots as a small electrical repair shop. Founded in 1961 in Sekinchan, Selangor, Khind began as a modest electrical repair business before gradually expanding through product innovation, distribution growth and brand development into a home solutions company. The company, operated by the Cheng family and listed on Bursa Malaysia as Khind Holdings Bhd, said the milestone reflects decades of transformation driven by continuous innovation in household appliances and customer-focused solutions.

items.title: Zahid welcomes new political party, urges unity
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has welcomed the formation of a new Malay-based political party, expressing hope that it would strengthen unity rather than divide the community. He said any individual or political figure was free to establish a new political platform, provided it contributed towards uniting Malays and advancing national interests. He added that Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative had successfully contributed to fostering national unity.

items.title: Blood, sweat and tears — and lost cash: KL police tracing mule accounts in BTS ticket scam probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police are tracing several bank accounts believed to have been used as mule accounts in connection with alleged fraud involving the sale of tickets for BTS’ upcoming concert in Malaysia. According to Berita Harian , Brickfields district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said investigations were ongoing and several bank accounts suspected of being involved had already been identified. Hoo said no new police reports had been lodged in relation to the case so far.

items.title: BTS ticket scam: Police track suspected mule bank accounts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracing several bank accounts believed to have been used as mule accounts in scams involving ticket sales for South Korean K-pop supergroup BTS's concert in December. Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said investigations were ongoing, with several bank accounts identified so far. "It is possible that these bank accounts are mule accounts," he said when contacted today.

items.title: Rosmah lodges police report over US$13mil luxury mansion purchase claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has lodged a police report over social media allegations linking her to the purported purchase of a US$13 million luxury property for her son-in-law. The posts, said to have been circulated on X and Facebook, claimed the wife of jailed former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak was involved in financing the acquisition of the property. Rosmah denied all the allegations, describing them as baseless and unsupported by credible evidence.

items.title: RM10mil allocated for programme to support 1,300 retiring armed forces personnel
items.summary: SEGAMAT: The Defence Ministry (Mindef) has allocated RM10 million this year for the implementation of its Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme, benefiting about 1,300 Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) personnel approaching retirement. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the allocation for the intervention programme forms part of the ministry's efforts to safeguard the welfare and livelihood of military veterans. He said the WBL programme enables the experience and expertise acquired by personnel throughout their service in the MAF to be formally assessed and recognised in collaboration with higher education institutions.

items.title: Zaid Ibrahim vows to reshape Pas image, says party can lead fairer Malaysia
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Former de facto law minister Datuk Zaid Ibrahim has pledged to help reshape public perception of Pas, describing the party as capable of governing a multicultural Malaysia based on justice and equality. Zaid, who officially joined Pas together with 13 other political figures ahead of the Negri Sembilan state election, said he was warmly received by the party's top leadership when he was admitted as a member last night. Also joining Pas are former Perak state executive councillor and former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Hamidah Othman, and former Negeri Sembilan Amanah chairman Dr Rosli Yaakup, who is also a former Pas commissioner for the state.

items.title: Police identify two bodies found in Kedah River, rule out link between deaths
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 14 — Police have identified one of two men whose bodies were recovered from the Kedah River today as a 16-year-old boy who had been reported missing two days earlier, adding that there was no indication the deaths were linked. Harian Metro reported Kota Setar police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali as saying that the teenager, identified as Saw Chee Wan, was believed to have left his home on June 12. Although the two bodies were found about 100 metres apart, police said investigations showed the cases were unrelated.

items.title: Loke: Onn Hafiz has attended Transport Ministry functions many times before
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has hit back at Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's claims about not being invited to Transport Ministry events. The transport minister said he and his party harboured no ill will towards Onn Hafiz despite recent exchanges between the two. He said DAP would not force anyone to work with the party.

items.title: Tight housing market boosts New York office conversions
items.summary: NEW YORK: New York real estate firms are increasingly converting office buildings into apartment complexes as an antidote to the city's housing crunch, tapping into tax incentives also designed to increase the supply of affordable homes. "My belief is just about any building could be converted as residential," Joey Chilelli, partner at real estate developer Vanbarton Group, told AFP during a tour of an office high-rise at 77 Water Street the firm is converting into a residential building. Real estate firms view the projects as less risky and quicker to implement than many new builds.

items.title: Drones more effective than boat patrols along Sungai Golok, say Kelantan police
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 14 — The use of drones is more effective than conventional boat patrols in curbing smuggling activities along the Sungai Golok border, as syndicates can easily detect patrol boats operating in the narrow river, Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said. He said the movement and engine noise of Marine Police patrol boats can be heard from across the border, allowing smugglers to suspend their activities before enforcement personnel can take action. Mohd Yusoff said 300 General Operations Force (GOF) personnel are deployed each month to monitor the 91-kilometre border stretch through 17 control posts.

items.title: Two decades later, infamous Highland Towers to be demolished by year's end
items.summary: Council deputy president Hasrolnizam Shaari said the court's decision paves the way for the long-abandoned structure to be torn down after more than two decades. He said the council is now in the process of coordinating the next steps especially to the parties involved in the demolition work. He said the council carries out various activities, including recycling campaigns, community clean-ups, tree-planting activities and environmental education initiatives that support the sustainable development agenda within its administrative area.

items.title: Tropicana strengthens ESG governance with MACC anti-corruption initiative
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tropicana Corp Bhd is reinforcing its commitment to environmental, social and governance (ESG) excellence through a strategic collaboration with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) aimed at strengthening integrity and governance practices across the organisation. The property developer recently hosted a MACC-led anti-corruption workshop on integrity or ethical conduct and best governance practices, covering key corruption-prevention topics. Tropicana head of ESG unit Serena Lim said the company remains committed to continuously strengthening its ESG and governance practices in line with evolving practices and regulatory requirements.

items.title: Court ruling clears way for Highland Towers demolition before year end, says MPAJ
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The remaining blocks of the Highland Towers condominium in Hulu Kelang are expected to be demolished before the end of the year, following a Magistrates’ Court decision allowing the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council (MPAJ) to proceed with the long-delayed plan. MPAJ deputy president Hasrolnizam Shaari said the court approved the council’s application last week, paving the way for the demolition of the abandoned structures that have stood vacant for more than three decades. Within this year, the demolition process will take place,” he told Harian Metro today.

items.title: Man detained for buying fuel twice within minutes at Tumpat petrol station
items.summary: Kelantan Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry director Hanif Yusabra Yusuf said the suspect was detained after investigations showed him buying 120 litres of petrol at the same petrol station. Hanif said early investigations found that the petrol station had allegedly breached Control of Supplies Regulations 1974 by carrying out repeated sales to the same customer. He said the petrol station operator is being investigated under Regulation 12A of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974.

items.title: Indonesian economy comes up for air but struggles to win back investors
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's economy faces a perfect storm wrought by high energy prices, and while the currency has rebounded slightly, critics warn government policies are unnerving investors at a critical moment. However, "investor concerns over recent domestic policy moves will persist", said an analysis by BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions. - 'Populist and interventionist' - High lending rates tend to dampen economic growth.

items.title: Anwar: Just poverty alleviation must follow need, not race or state lines
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for a deeper understanding of what justice and compassion truly means in the context of poverty alleviation. Speaking to reporters after officiating the Koperasi Profesional Putrajaya Bhd (Koprojaya) 10th annual general meeting, he said the political discourse in the country had too often reduced the question of justice to one of race or geography. Anwar said, while the majority of those living in poverty in Malaysia were still Malay Bumiputera, significant pockets of poverty also existed within the Indian community and among the Orang Asli.

items.title: UAE AI push opens Gulf opportunities for Malaysian firms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The United Arab Emirates (UAE), one of the Middle East's leading artificial intelligence (AI) investment hubs, is channelling billions of dollars into digital infrastructure, sovereign cloud systems and smart city initiatives as it accelerates efforts to diversify its economy and strengthen long-term digital competitiveness. The rapid expansion of the UAE's digital ecosystem is expected to create opportunities for Malaysian companies to participate in AI-ready data centre projects, smart infrastructure and digital sovereignty initiatives, particularly as Gulf governments ramp up spending on advanced technologies. Held in Abu Dhabi on June 11, the summit brought together senior government officials, regulators and technology executives to discuss investments in AI, data infrastructure, cloud systems and smart technologies amid rising regional demand for digital transformation.

items.title: Selangor Bomba: Four women rescued after losing their way on Bukit Tunggul hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Four women who lost their way while hiking at Bukit Tunggul in Dengkil were found safe and escorted out by firefighters today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said authorities received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the group had become disoriented during the hike. “All four women were located by firefighters and escorted to a safe area.

items.title: Two bodies found 100m apart in Kedah River near Alor Setar bridge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The bodies of two males, aged 16 and 46, were found in the Kedah River near the Jeragan Waterfront Bridge in Alor Setar today. Kedah Fire and Rescue Department Zone 1 chief Ahmad Aminuddin Abd Rahim said firefighters were first alerted at 8.45am after receiving a report of a body caught among debris in the river. Following checks carried out with police officers, firefighters retrieved the second body from the river.

items.title: Fuziah: PKR will contest 20 seats in Johor, 16 in Negri Sembilan
items.summary: KUANTAN: PKR will contest at least 20 seats in Johor and 16 seats in the Negri Sembilan elections, said its secretary-general, Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh. PH had previously announced that it will contest all 56 seats in the Johor election and all 36 seats in Negri Sembilan. Meanwhile, Fuziah said she expects voter turnout for the Johor election to increase compared with the previous state election, which was held when the country was still affected by Covid-19.

items.title: Zahid says new Malay parties must unite, not divide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said new Malay-based political parties were welcome, provided they helped unite rather than further divide the community. Speaking after the Majlis Zikir Peringatan Haul Asmaulhusna 2026 at Masjid Sultan Iskandar in Bandar Dato’ Onn, Johor Bahru, today, the Barisan Nasional chairman said any individual or political figure was free to establish a new party as long as it helped unite Malays rather than deepen existing divisions. “Whatever platform is used, if in the end we are able to unite our actions around the national agenda, the Islamic agenda and the agenda of our community, insya-Allah.

items.title: Kinabatangan and Crocker Range biosphere reserves important for Sabah
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The proclamation of Kinabatangan Biosphere Reserve marks a historic moment and a proud achievement for Sabah and Malaysia. "With this world-class status, the Kinabatangan Biosphere Reserve is expected to attract international attention in the fields of research, education, nature-based tourism and global collaboration opportunities," he said. Hajiji was speaking at the 12th anniversary celebration of the Crocker Range Biosphere Reserve and the declaration ceremony of the Kinabatangan Biosphere Reserve at Padang Merdeka here.

items.title: Rosmah denies role in alleged US$13m property deal, lodges police report over online claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has denied allegations linking her and her family to an alleged US$13 million (RM53 million) property transaction, saying she had lodged a police report and would pursue legal action against those responsible for disseminating the claims online. In a statement issued today in response to videos circulating on social media, the wife of former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak said she had no involvement, either directly or indirectly, in the acquisition, transfer or ownership of any luxury property, including one allegedly valued at US$13 million. Rosmah said the videos had harmed her family’s reputation.

items.title: Police detain man suspected of abusing stepson
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Police have arrested a man on suspicion of abusing his 2-year-old stepson here yesterday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Yatim Osman said police received a report on the incident from the child's biological mother about 8.30pm before arresting the 24-year-old suspect in front of Tuanku Ja'afar Hospital (HTJ) about two hours later. Meanwhile, he said checks found that the man had no prior criminal record and had tested negative in a preliminary urine screening.

items.title: IAEA chief: Malaysia on solid footing to pursue nuclear power
items.summary: VIENNA: Malaysia is well-positioned to pursue a nuclear power programme, having built a long-standing tradition and expertise in nuclear technology, International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) director-general Rafael Mariano Grossi said. He said Malaysia's experience in operating research reactor has provided the country with a strong foundation to advance its nuclear energy ambition as part of its future power mix. "There are many people in Malaysia who know nuclear technology and know what a nuclear reactor is," he added.

items.title: Sisters and their husbands held over viral video of maid abuse
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Two married couples were arrested yesterday to facilitate investigations after a video showing three domestic helpers being assaulted in Taman Johor near Tampoi went viral on social media. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the couples, aged between 30 and 34, were arrested at a house in the residential area about 7.30pm by Johor Criminal Investigation Department and Johor Bahru Utara police headquarters policemen. Meanwhile, Rahaman said police had seized mobile phones, clothing belonging to the suspects, a closed-circuit television (CCTV) camera and two domestic helpers' passports.

items.title: Two married couples held over viral video of domestic helper assault, says Johor police chief
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 14 — Two married couples were arrested yesterday to assist in investigations after a video showing domestic helpers being assaulted in Taman Johor near Tampoi went viral on social media. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the couples, aged between 30 and 34, were arrested at a house in the residential area at about 7.30 pm by personnel from the Johor Criminal Investigation Department (CID) and the Johor Bahru Utara district police headquarters. Meanwhile, Ab Rahaman said police had seized mobile phones, clothing belonging to the suspects, a closed-circuit television (CCTV) camera and the two domestic helpers’ passports.

items.title: Seremban police detain man suspected of abusing stepson
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 14 — Police have arrested a man on suspicion of abusing his two-year-old stepson here yesterday. Seremban district police chief ACP Mohd Yatim Osman said police received a report on the incident from the child's biological mother at about 8.30pm before arresting the 24-year-old suspect in front of Tuanku Ja'afar Hospital (HTJ) about two hours later. Mohd Yatim said the couple had only recently married, tying the knot in April this year.

items.title: IAEA chief: Malaysia on solid footing to pursue nuclear power
items.summary: VIENNA, June 14 — Malaysia is well-positioned to pursue a nuclear power programme, having built a long-standing tradition and expertise in nuclear technology, International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi said. He said Malaysia’s experience in operating research reactor has provided the country with a strong foundation to advance its nuclear energy ambition as part of its future power mix. “There are many people in Malaysia who know nuclear technology and know what a nuclear reactor is,” he added.

items.title: Tuan Ibrahim: More prominent figures expected to join Pas soon
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Pas expects more prominent personalities to join the party following the admission of 14 new members, including several former ministers, political leaders and professionals last night. Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said those who have expressed their intentions to join up were from various backgrounds. Among the prominent figures were former Negri Sembilan state executive councillor and Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Dr Mohamad Rafie Abdul Malek, former Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law) Datuk Zaid Ibrahim, and former private secretary to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Rahimi Osman.

items.title: Anwar: Attempts to topple stable Negeri Sembilan govt amount to ‘a degree of betrayal’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said attempts to bring down the Negeri Sembilan government despite political and economic stability amounted to “a degree of betrayal”. Asked about recent claims of betrayal linked to discussions on electoral arrangements among coalition partners, Anwar, who’s also the Pakatan Harapan chairman, said such concerns would not arise if there was no betrayal. Anwar also urged political parties to focus on economic growth and issues affecting the public instead of becoming consumed by election speculation.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Efforts to topple Negri government a form of betrayal
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has maintained that efforts to destabilise the Negri Sembilan government amounted to a form of betrayal, even as Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi rejected suggestions that Umno had acted disloyally. Anwar said his position was based on what he saw as deliberate attempts to undermine a government that had been functioning in conditions of political and economic stability. Yesterday, Zahid, who is also Umno president, rejected claims that the party had betrayed its allies in Negri Sembilan, saying there was no question of disloyalty once a government's term had ended.

items.title: Operation to capture tiger near Gerik kampung snares 11 elephants instead
items.summary: GERIK: A wildlife operation initially triggered by reports of tiger attacks in Kampung Bongor took an unexpected turn, with authorities capturing 11 wild elephants after the animals moved into village areas and damaged crops. Kampung Bongor Village Development and Security Committee chairman Mohamad Ghazali Salleh said the presence of elephants was first detected on Thursday night when villagers and officers from the Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) were attempting to set up camera traps near a rubber plantation in the village. "Three elephants were captured that night, and up until today, 11 have been successfully captured.

items.title: Continuous border enforcement cuts cross-border smuggling, says Kelantan Domestic Trade chief
items.summary: TUMPAT: Round-the-clock surveillance at Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complexes, coupled with stringent inspections of modified vehicles, has helped prevent the smuggling of subsidised fuel and other controlled goods into neighbouring countries. Kelantan Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry director Hanif Yusabra Yusuf said the continuous cooperation among enforcement agencies had contributed to a decline in cross-border smuggling activities involving subsidised goods. "Smuggling of subsidised goods has decreased compared with previous years due to the commitment shown by all enforcement agencies.

items.title: Five arrested after men lured through fake Grindr profiles lose RM315,000 in cash and jewellery, Johor police say
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Five men have been arrested over an alleged robbery scheme in which victims were lured through fake profiles on the dating app Grindr before being threatened and relieved of their valuables, Johor police said today. Johor police chief Comm Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the suspects, aged between 21 and 28, were believed to have targeted men by posing as potential romantic partners and arranging meetings through the social media platform. Ab Rahaman said victims were persuaded to meet the suspects for dates before being brought into a vehicle.

items.title: Between Trump and a hard place: Fed chair Warsh to lead first rate meeting
items.summary: US Federal Reserve chief Kevin Warsh will chair his first meeting of the central bank's rate-setting committee next week caught between a rock and a hard place. Inflation is at a three-year high but Warsh still faces unrelenting pressure from the White House to lower interest rates. The bank's 12-member Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) will begin a two-day meeting on Tuesday and is widely expected to hold rates steady as the effects of US President Donald Trump's war on Iran course through the world's largest economy.

items.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday, Hormuz to open after
items.summary: US President Donald Trump said that a deal with Iran to end the war in the Middle East could be signed Sunday, and that the strategic Strait of Hormuz would be "open to all" immediately after. The leader of key mediator Pakistan said a deal was closer "than ever before". A Pakistani foreign ministry statement also said the signing was planned for Sunday.

items.title: Dengue cases up 27pc nationwide, with Sabah among the hardest hit, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Dengue cases nationwide rose 27 per cent to 33,367 as of June 13 (Epidemiological Week 23), compared with 27,640 cases recorded during the corresponding period last year. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said the situation in Sabah was more concerning, with the state recording a sharp 50.4 per cent increase to 2,866 cases, compared with 1,905 cases during the same period last year. He said several districts in Sabah had been identified as the main contributors to the state’s caseload, namely Kota Kinabalu, Kota Marudu, Tawau, Sandakan, Penampang and Putatan.

items.title: Activist to make 290km Taiping-Parliament run for 3 elephants in Japan
items.summary: IPOH: Animal welfare advocate Neow Choo Seong will undertake a 290km solo run from Taiping to Parliament in Kuala Lumpur in a bid to draw greater attention to the welfare and future of three elephants currently housed in Japan. Describing Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK) as part of Malaysia's natural heritage, Neow said the six-day endurance challenge aims to raise public awareness and galvanise support for efforts to bring the elephants home. "Dara, Amoi and Kelat are part of Malaysia's natural heritage.

items.title: Three factory buses destroyed in Serendah industrial area fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three factory buses were destroyed in a fire at an industrial area in Taman Melati Desa Kiambang in Serendah early today. "The fire involved three factory buses parked within a factory compound. "Ten firefighters, assisted by a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) from Kuala Kubu Baru and a Compact Fire Rescue Tender (CFRT) from Bukit Sentosa, were involved in the operation," he said.

items.title: ‘No buyers, no sellers’: Sarawak deputy minister calls for crackdown on purchasers of stolen cables
items.summary: KUCHING, June 14 — Efforts to curb vandalism and cable theft affecting public facilities must go beyond arresting perpetrators and focus on those who purchase stolen cables, said Datuk Ibrahim Baki. He said this when met after officiating a smart street lighting project at Kampung Nombor Satok here on Friday. Ibrahim pointed out that cable theft continues to affect public infrastructure and utility facilities, resulting not only in financial losses but also posing safety risks to the public.

items.title: Firefighters race to locate four women lost on Bukit Tunggul
items.summary: SEPANG: Firefighters are searching for four women who became lost while hiking at Bukit Tunggul in Lorong Bahagia, Jenderam Hilir, near here today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the hikers had lost their way while descending the hill. Mukhlis said six firefighters, led by operations commander Muhamad Sakirin Selamat, were involved in the operation.

items.title: Only RM1,000 returned after Kuching retiree ‘invests’ RM9m in alleged online IPO scam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — A 79-year-old man in Kuching has lost more than RM9 million after allegedly falling victim to a fraudulent online investment scheme involving non-existent share investments and initial public offerings (IPOs). Kuching police chief Assistant Commissioner Alexson Naga Chabu said the district police headquarters’ Commercial Crime Investigation Division had recently received a report from the victim, who suffered losses amounting to RM9.08 million. According to Alexson, the victim was persuaded to participate in the purported share investment and IPO scheme and subsequently made 27 separate transactions into nine different bank accounts provided by the suspect.

items.title: Bukit Aman warns those caught with child sexual abuse material may escalate to more serious sexual offences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police have warned that individuals who access or possess online child sexual abuse material (CSAM) may escalate to more serious offences, including grooming, sextortion and sexual crimes against children. She said repeated exposure to CSAM could lead to behavioural addiction, a condition in which individuals are compelled to seek more content and spend longer periods accessing such material. Siti Kamsiah said investigations had found that online communities sharing the material typically operated within closed groups, whose members often did not know one another personally.

items.title: Not your usual roadside hazard: Three-metre crocodile rescued from Banting drain
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said firefighters were alerted to the unusual reptile rescue at 9.07am. A team from the Banting Fire and Rescue Station rushed to the scene, where they discovered the hefty crocodile trapped in the drain near the road. The rescue operation involved eight firefighters and two fire engines.

items.title: PAS says Hamzah Zainudin’s return as Opposition Leader was always the plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — PAS said Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's return as Opposition Leader had long been its preferred outcome, adding that Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia had previously been uncomfortable with him remaining in the role following his expulsion from the party. Berita Harian reported that PAS deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the Islamist party had wanted to retain Hamzah in the role from the outset because of his performance and the support he commanded among Opposition lawmakers. “So when he formed a new party (Parti Wawasan Negara) and joined us (PAS), we thought we should continue to retain him (as Opposition Leader),” Tuan Ibrahim told reporters after a pre-election ceramah at the PAS operations centre in Seremban last night.

items.title: PKR’s Bukit Batu candidate Arthur Chiong apologises over incorrect claim on Johor allocations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — PKR’s candidate for the Bukit Batu state seat, Arthur Chiong Sen Sern, has apologised to the Johor government after making an incorrect claim during his announcement as the coalition’s candidate last night. Chiong, who is also the incumbent Bukit Batu assemblyman, said he was withdrawing remarks made during the launch of the Bukit Batu election machinery. Yesterday, Pakatan Harapan secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail announced Chiong as the coalition’s candidate to defend the Bukit Batu seat in the upcoming Johor state election.

items.title: Three-hour trek in, 15 hours out: Firefighters carry injured Fraser’s Hill hiker to safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — A woman injured during a hike to Twin Peak in Fraser’s Hill was rescued after firefighters carried her through rugged jungle terrain in an operation that stretched for 15 hours. According to New Straits Times , the 43-year-old suffered a suspected fracture to her right leg while trekking between Hobbit Cave and the Pine Tree Hill trail en route to Twin Peak, which rises 1,505 metres above sea level. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said a distress call was received at 12.15pm yesterday, prompting the deployment of six firefighters from the Raub station and a mountain guide.

items.title: Malaysia weathers 100 days of global energy crisis, supplies remain stable
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 14 — Malaysia remains in a relatively strong position with sufficient supplies of fuel, food and essential medicines, thanks to early intervention and coordinated crisis management efforts, says Prime Minister’s Economic Adviser Nurhisham Hussein. Nurhisham, who also heads the Secretariat of the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) Crisis Management Task Force, said the government’s immediate priority since the crisis began has been ensuring uninterrupted supplies of critical goods and services. Energy, healthcare supplies remain secure On the energy front, Nurhisham said Malaysia’s electricity generation capacity remains sufficient despite heightened global energy market volatility.

items.title: Thousands of Malaysians continue to visit southern Thailand despite safety warnings, says Kelantan police chief
items.summary: Revealing this concerning trend, Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said that daily observations at the state’s three official entry points (ICQS Complexes) — Rantau Panjang in Pasir Mas, Pengkalan Kubor in Tumpat, and Bukit Bunga in Tanah Merah — have recorded extremely high cross-border traffic. He said that on normal days, an estimated 3,000 people cross the border, while the figure rises to more than 4,000 during weekends. “Overall, the total number of entries and exits involving border passes or passports can reach as high as 10,000 people in a single day.

items.title: PTPTN eKasih Children’s Scholarship allocation increased to RM210m, will benefit 10,000 students
items.summary: KULAI, June 14 — The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated RM210 million under the PTPTN eKasih Children’s Scholarship (PTPTN BAeK), benefiting 10,000 students from poor and hardcore poor families. Also present were PTPTN chairman Datuk Seri Norliza Abdul Rahim, chief executive Ahmad Dasuki Abdul Majid and Johor PTPTN State Office director Mazni Ruslan. Norliza said students must first apply for a PTPTN loan through the myPTPTN web application.

items.title: Loke renews KTM Komuter invitation to Onn Hafiz: ‘See you in Kulai on Tuesday’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke today defended his ministry’s engagement with the Johor government, citing several transport-related events attended by Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi as he renewed his invitation for the latter to join Tuesday’s KTM Komuter Selatan launch. The DAP secretary-general’s response came after Onn Hafiz said he had yet to receive a formal invitation to the event despite being “touched” by Loke’s public invitation to ride the train service together. Loke then listed several Ministry of Transport (MOT) programmes that Onn Hafiz had attended personally.

items.title: Hadi: PAS supports Parti Wawasan Negara joining Perikatan, Bersatu free to decide its future
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — PAS yesterday endorsed Parti Wawasan Negara’s bid to join Perikatan Nasional (PN), as party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia was free to decide whether it wanted to remain in the Opposition coalition. “PAS supports and accepts Parti Wawasan Negara joining PN. “PAS agrees to accept Parti Wawasan Negara into PN because PAS is open.

items.title: Women are pillars of a Madani nation, says PM
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said women are indispensable to Malaysia’s development as a Madani nation, urging them to anchor the country’s progress in values, character and faith. “Without the contribution of women, there is no Madani nation that we hope for, one that is fresh and flourishing,” he said at the State-level Melangkah Bersama Madani: Wanita Beriman, Wanita Bertahan Programme at the Sabah International Convention Centre, yesterday. This is Madani,” he said.

items.title: No hydrochloric acid contamination detected in Sungai Cherating after tanker spill, says Pahang DOE
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 14 — The Pahang Department of Environment (DOE) said no acid contamination had been detected in Sungai Cherating following a hydrochloric acid spill from an overturned tanker near the Cherating Bridge yesterday. The department said the accident site was about 100 metres from the river. “Meanwhile, pH monitoring using YSI equipment in Sungai Cherating recorded a reading of 9.4, indicating that no acid contamination had occurred in the river,” the DOE said in a statement today.

items.title: Police detain lorry driver over viral Kajang SILK Highway clip showing reckless driving, obscene gesture
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police have arrested a lorry driver after a video appearing to show him driving dangerously and making an obscene gesture along the Kajang SILK Highway towards Bandar Baru Bangi went viral on TikTok. The 35-year-old local man was detained by officers from the Criminal Investigation Division at the Kajang district police headquarters at about 8.45pm yesterday to assist investigations. Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said police became aware of the footage before a report was lodged at the Kajang police station at 7.15pm.

items.title: Form Two student killed in Alor Gajah crash involving Perodua Viva and tanker lorry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — A 14-year-old student was killed after the motorcycle he was riding collided with a car before he was struck by a tanker lorry in Lubok China, Melaka, police said. Alor Gajah district police chief Superintendent Ahmad Abu Bakar said preliminary investigations found that the teenager had been riding a Yamaha LC motorcycle from his home in Kampung Sungai Siput Batu Alang towards Lubok China. “The victim was confirmed dead at the scene, while the 32-year-old car driver and the 43-year-old tanker lorry driver were unhurt,” he said in a statement today.
